# GithubSource class

Namespace: [`Velopack.Sources`](./index.md)

Retrieves available releases from a GitHub repository.

```cs
public class GithubSource : GitBase<GithubRelease>, IUpdateSource
```

**Inherits:** `GitBase<GithubRelease>`  
**Implements:** [`IUpdateSource`](./IUpdateSource.md)  

## Constructors

| Constructor | Summary |
| --- | --- |
| [`GithubSource`](#githubsource-string-string-bool-ifiledownloader) | Retrieves available releases from a GitHub repository. |


### GithubSource(repoUrl, accessToken, prerelease, downloader) {#githubsource-string-string-bool-ifiledownloader}

```cs
public GithubSource(string repoUrl, string? accessToken, bool prerelease, IFileDownloader? downloader = null)
```

Retrieves available releases from a GitHub repository.

**Parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `repoUrl` | `string` | The URL of the GitHub repository to download releases from <br/>(e.g. https://github.com/myuser/myrepo) |
| `accessToken` | `string` | The GitHub access token to use with the request to download releases. <br/>If left empty, the GitHub rate limit for unauthenticated requests allows <br/>for up to 60 requests per hour, limited by IP address. |
| `prerelease` | `bool` | If true, pre-releases will be also be searched / downloaded. If false, only<br/>stable releases will be considered. |
| `downloader` | [`IFileDownloader`](./IFileDownloader.md) | The file downloader used to perform HTTP requests. |

### GetAssetUrlFromName(release, assetName) {#getasseturlfromname-githubrelease-string}

```cs
protected override string GetAssetUrlFromName(GithubRelease release, string assetName)
```

Given a [`GithubRelease`](./GithubRelease.md) and an asset filename (eg. 'RELEASES') this 
function will return either `BrowserDownloadUrl` or
`Url`, depending whether an access token is available
or not. Throws if the specified release has no matching assets.

**Parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `release` | [`GithubRelease`](./GithubRelease.md) |  |
| `assetName` | `string` |  |


**Returns** `string`

### GetApiBaseUrl(repoUrl) {#getapibaseurl-uri}

```cs
protected virtual Uri GetApiBaseUrl(Uri repoUrl)
```

Given a repository URL (e.g. https://github.com/myuser/myrepo) this function
returns the API base for performing requests. (eg. "https://api.github.com/" 
or http://internal.github.server.local/api/v3)

**Parameters**

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `repoUrl` | `Uri` |  |


**Returns** `Uri`
